records.ety: [F. virer (cf. Sp. virar, birar), LL. virare; perhaps fr. L. vibrare to brandish, vibrate (cf. Vibrate); or cf. L. viriae armlets, bracelets, viriola a little bracelet (cf. Ferrule). Cf. Environ.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Veered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Veering.]

defs: To change direction; to turn; to shift; as, wind veers to the west or north.
quotes: And as he leads, the following navy veers.
an ordinary community which is hostile or friendly as passion or as interest may veer about.

defs: To direct to a different course; to turn; to wear; as, to veer, or wear, a vessel.
